segments.text: Then the whole congregation of Israel left the Desert of Sin, moving from place to place as the LORD commanded. They camped at Rephidim, but there was no water for the people to drink.
So the people contended with Moses,
Give us water to drink.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Why do you contend with me?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Moses replied.
Why do you test the LORD?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But the people thirsted for water there, and they grumbled against Moses:
Why have you brought us out of Egypt—to make us and our children and livestock die of thirst?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Then Moses cried out to the LORD,
What should I do with these people? A little more and they will stone me!
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: And the LORD said to Moses,
Walk on ahead of the people and take some of the elders of Israel with you. Take along in your hand the staff with which you struck the Nile, and go.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Behold, I will st and there before you by the rock at Horeb. And when you strike the rock, water will come out of it for the people to drink.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So Moses did this in the sight of the elders of Israel.
He named the place Massah and Meribah because the Israelites quarreled, and because they tested the LORD, saying,
Is the LORD among us or not?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: After this, the Amalekites came and attacked the Israelites at Rephidim.
So Moses said to Joshua,
Choose some of our men and go out to fight the Amalekites. Tomorrow I will st and on the hilltop with the staff of God in my hand.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Joshua did as Moses had instructed him and fought against the Amalekites, while Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
As long as Moses held up his hands, Israel prevailed; but when he lowered them, Amalek prevailed.

When Moses’ hands grew heavy, they took a stone and put it under him, and he sat on it. Then Aaron and Hur held his hands up, one on each side, so that his hands remained steady until the sun went down.
So Joshua overwhelmed Amalek and his army with the sword.
Then the LORD said to Moses,
Write this on a scroll as a reminder and recite it to Joshua, because I will utterly blot out the memory of Amalek from under heaven.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: And Moses built an altar and named it The LORD Is My Banner.
Indeed,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said,
a hand was lifted up toward the throne of the LORD. The LORD will war against Amalek from generation to generation.
segments.speaker: Unknown
